meta_title: For Students: marketing automation to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Marketing Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Learn how to build high-impact marketing automation tools using Antigravity and Claude Code in this beginner tutorial.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by shipping real utility. By building a marketing automation tool—such as an automated newsletter curator or lead scraper—you demonstrate technical competence and business value. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to iterate your logic, creating a project that recruiters actually value.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank GitHub profile, feeling the weight of the 'entry-level' paradox: you need experience to get a job, but you need a job to get experience. The silence of an empty repository is deafening, and every day you spend just watching tutorials is a day you aren't building the career you deserve.

But here is the secret: recruiters don't care about generic to-do lists. They care about systems that solve problems. Marketing automation is the ultimate 'proof of work' because it sits at the intersection of code, data, and revenue. Let’s stop building toys and start building engines.

steps.title: Define Your Automation Engine
steps.description: Before writing code, define the specific pain point. We are building a LinkedIn post analyzer that triggers an automated email summary.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a project architecture for a LinkedIn data scraper that filters posts by keywords and saves them to a CSV file.

steps.title: Initialize the Scraper Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to establish the connection layer between your script and the web target.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust Python function using BeautifulSoup to extract post text, author, and timestamp from a provided HTML snippet.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create scraper.py with error handling for dynamic web elements'

steps.title: Implement the Automation Trigger
steps.description: Transform data into action by setting up a scheduled task that processes the scraped content.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a logic flow that checks if the scraped post contains specific technical keywords and triggers an alert.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add logic to filter posts based on a list of technical tags'

steps.title: Connect the Notification API
steps.description: Automate the delivery of your data so the system 'works' while you sleep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a module to send an automated email notification using SMTP or a service like Resend whenever a relevant post is found.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate email notification service with environment variable configuration'

steps.title: Deploy and Document
steps.description: A project without a README is invisible. Package your code for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md that highlights the problem, the solution, the tech stack, and how to run the automation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a high-quality README.md that emphasizes the business value of this tool'

pro_tips: Always focus on the 'Business Logic'—recruiters look for how you handle API rate limits and data sanitization, not just syntax.
Use environment variables (.env) for every single key; never hardcode credentials, as this is the #1 red flag in code reviews.
Add a 'Future Improvements' section to your README; it shows architectural foresight and professional maturity.
Record a 60-second Loom video demoing the automation in action; it is worth more than 1,000 lines of code on a resume.
Build 'defensive' code by adding try-except blocks around every network request to ensure your automation never crashes silently.
content.next_steps: Once your automation is live, deploy it to a free tier on Render or Railway, add the link to your LinkedIn profile header, and reach out to one person in your target industry asking for feedback on the tool.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with a live, functional product. This is the difference between being ignored and being hired. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
